costDrivers: repository-wide discovery was needed once to locate existing token, review, retrospective, and automation docs
avoidableCosts: future tasks should start from PROJECT_EXPERIENCE_GUIDE.md and PROJECT_REVIEW_SYSTEM.md instead of broad retrospective scans
productLessons: project retrospectives are governance and execution learning, not PRD authority
technicalLessons: append-only jsonl keeps task retrospectives cheap to add and easy to aggregate
designLessons: UI/UX lessons should capture interaction and visual evidence quality, not only token cost
processLessons: weekly summaries should read review docs and the ledger, then promote only repeated lessons
tokenLessons: token usage must be recorded as a cost signal but interpreted against task value and outcome
avoidNextTime: do not reduce project retrospectives to token optimization only

costDrivers: first retrospective intentionally performed a broad repository document scan
recent review statistics required inspecting review filenames and repeated evidence phrases
avoidableCosts: future weekly reviews should start from docs/retrospectives/weekly/2026-W28.md and only inspect new reviews since the previous retrospective
adjacent visual-only UI work should be batched into owner-visible bundles instead of creating a full review cycle for every small surface change
productLessons: the useful docs are the routing docs and failure retrospectives, not additional parallel plans
technicalLessons: P6 retrospectives remain the best anti-pattern checklist for evidence and integration design
designLessons: UI/UX polish needs bundle-level foreground acceptance planning to avoid repeating smoke-vs-foreground disclaimers
processLessons: review cadence should distinguish micro-implementation slices from meaningful checkpoint packages
tokenLessons: a wide scan was justified once to build the source map, but repeating it would be low value
avoidNextTime: do not rescan all historical docs for ordinary implementation tasks
do not let high review-file count substitute for visible product closure

costDrivers: first expanded baseline intentionally scanned project history from inception, docs inventory, review inventory, loop history, stage reports, product docs, research docs, and recurring review patterns
avoidableCosts: future historical grounding should start from docs/retrospectives/PROJECT_BASELINE_RETROSPECTIVE.md instead of rescanning the whole repository
ordinary implementation tasks should load only authority docs, the lane doc, the experience guide, and one or two matching historical retrospectives
productLessons: write owner resets into authority docs quickly and isolate old lanes so historical scope does not leak back into the active product surface
technicalLessons: keep source-of-truth contracts, preview/export semantics, binary validity, visual playback, and owner acceptance as separate states
designLessons: batch adjacent visual-only UI/UX polish into surface-level acceptance bundles with one foreground evidence path
processLessons: review volume can become a cost center when every tiny neighboring change gets its own full review cycle
large milestones should be owned by vertical user flows rather than technical layers
tokenLessons: a broad scan has high value once for baseline creation but low value if repeated as routine task setup
avoidNextTime: do not treat review-count growth as product progress
do not reload every historical document when a baseline router already exists

costDrivers: 15 Figma screenshot reads were needed to archive stable visual targets for the main short-term UI states
rate-limit spacing added elapsed time but avoided read-limit and retry risk
productLessons: Figma design screenshots are implementation targets and evidence inputs, not PRD authority or Owner acceptance
technicalLessons: rendered screenshot dimensions can include window/shadow treatment and differ from first inventory frame dimensions
designLessons: contact-sheet visual QA quickly confirms that a screenshot archive covers the intended state set before UI code changes
processLessons: separate R1 screenshot evidence from later token and component reads so quota attribution and task review stay clean
tokenLessons: pilot-first screenshot capture can prevent quota waste when archive/download assumptions are unproven
avoidNextTime: do not batch many Figma screenshots before proving URL download and local hash recording work

costDrivers: initial all-token Figma read was truncated and required split collection reads
mapping had to compare 95 Figma variables against the existing 480-definition CSS token layer
avoidableCosts: future Figma variable extraction should split by collection from the start
productLessons: Figma tokens are implementation inputs and do not override the PRD
technicalLessons: the current CSS token layer has many component tokens but lacks Figma-aligned base palette tokens and full spacing/radius scales
designLessons: high-fidelity visual polish should start after base and semantic token drift is resolved deliberately
processLessons: keep R2 documentation-only so truncation recovery and token-map review stay clean
tokenLessons: return compact values from Figma variables: hex colors, alias IDs/names, and no unnecessary codeSyntax or key fields
avoidNextTime: do not request all Figma variables with full metadata in one response

costDrivers: Owner clarification required revising R3 and R4 before more Figma reads
the existing plan still treated component reading as a flat component index plus component contract list
avoidableCosts: future component reads should not start from flat component-library scans when an atomic hierarchy exists
productLessons: component hierarchy changes implementation read strategy but does not change short-term product scope
technicalLessons: Figma component reads should capture stable contracts and dependencies rather than every descendant layer
designLessons: module-first reading preserves atomic design composition and reduces irrelevant atom and molecule reads
processLessons: Owner MCP authorization gates should be written into the read plan before calls are made
tokenLessons: classify hierarchy before dependency reads to reduce Figma quota and context waste
avoidNextTime: do not request full component-library descendant trees for an atomic design system

costDrivers: the R3 plan needed to incorporate the Owner-confirmed top-level Figma sections before another MCP call
avoidableCosts: future R3 reads should not infer atom molecule module hierarchy when explicit sections exist
productLessons: Figma section structure is implementation guidance and does not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: explicit section reads reduce payload ambiguity and make dependency mapping cheaper
designLessons: read order should follow the authored atomic-design hierarchy in the Figma file
processLessons: fold Owner-provided Figma organization updates into the read plan before calling MCP
tokenLessons: read known sections directly instead of spending context on classification inference
avoidNextTime: do not treat section naming as unknown after Owner confirms the Figma structure

costDrivers: R3 needed three MCP reads because the first read found title-case section names and the second richer read was truncated by the tool output channel
avoidableCosts: future component hierarchy reads should match section names case-insensitively and use ultra-compact output from the start
productLessons: Figma component hierarchy is implementation input and does not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: direct-child component maps can still exceed output limits if they include coordinates and redundant direct refs
designLessons: the component library now has an explicit Atom Molecule Module structure that supports module-first implementation
processLessons: hard-cap planning gave enough room to recover without exceeding Owner authorization
tokenLessons: reserve rich layout and descendant reads for one named module or molecule at a time
avoidNextTime: do not use rich component hierarchy output as the default R3 shape

costDrivers: R3b had to reconcile PRD work packages with the Figma module hierarchy before R4
avoidableCosts: future agents should not jump from hierarchy maps directly into broad component-detail reads
productLessons: Figma dependency routing supports implementation but does not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: a WP-to-module queue reduces the risk of reading low-priority component details
designLessons: module-first planning preserves the authored design-system composition
processLessons: insert no-MCP planning between expensive Figma read rounds when it materially narrows the next call
tokenLessons: local dependency planning can save multiple Figma MCP reads later
avoidNextTime: do not start R4 without a WP-to-module dependency queue

costDrivers: R2 token values needed to be reconciled with existing compatibility aliases and dark-mode overrides
avoidableCosts: future token extraction should include the target code check shape before implementation starts
productLessons: Figma tokens guide implementation but do not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: token reads become useful only after code variables and regression checks consume them
designLessons: page-level polish should build on a Figma-aligned token base instead of scattered local values
processLessons: no-MCP implementation slices can turn prior Figma reads into concrete project progress
tokenLessons: use completed Figma reads before requesting new Figma calls
avoidNextTime: do not leave extracted token maps as docs-only inputs
